title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: Eating for the Record: The World of Competitive Eating
content.text: On the fourth of July every year, a crowd gathers at Nathan's Famous hot dog stand on Coney Island, New York, for one of the most unlikely sporting spectacles in America. Competitors sit in front of towering piles of hot dogs and attempt to consume as many as possible in ten minutes. (37) [37] El ganador actual puede superar los setenta perritos calientes en ese tiempo, una cifra que desafía la imaginación. [/37] To most observers, it looks absurd. To its participants, it is a serious athletic pursuit demanding dedicated training and physical preparation. || Competitive eating has a surprisingly long history. Pie-eating contests and similar challenges were common at fairs and festivals across Europe and North America throughout the nineteenth century. (38) [38] Sin embargo, el deporte moderno organizado tal como lo conocemos surgió principalmente en los Estados Unidos en los años noventa. [/38] The founding of Major League Eating in the 1990s provided a professional structure, ranking system and prize money that transformed informal contests into a legitimate circuit. || The competitors themselves are often not what people expect. Many are slender, and some are women who outperform much larger male rivals. (39) [39] Los científicos se han interesado por las características físicas que permiten a estos competidores ingerir cantidades tan extremas. [/39] Research has shown that elite competitive eaters can expand their stomachs to a degree that most people cannot, a capacity that appears to be partly innate and partly developed through practice. || The training regimes are surprisingly rigorous. Competitors practise expanding their stomach capacity using large volumes of water or low-calorie foods such as watermelon, and work on jaw endurance to maintain the speed of consumption throughout a contest. (40) [40] La preparación mental es tan importante como la física para mantener el ritmo bajo presión. [/40] Top eaters also study their opponents' techniques and develop personalised strategies for different types of food. || Medical opinion on competitive eating is divided. Some doctors argue that repeatedly stretching the stomach beyond normal capacity risks permanent damage to digestive function. (41) [41] Otros señalan que no hay pruebas sólidas de daños a largo plazo en competidores veteranos y sanos. [/41] Proponents of the sport point out that athletes in many conventional disciplines — boxing, gymnastics, long-distance running — subject their bodies to stresses that outsiders might consider equally alarming. || Whatever its critics say, competitive eating shows no sign of losing its audience. (42) [42] Las transmisiones en línea han multiplicado su alcance, atrayendo millones de espectadores a nivel mundial. [/42] In the attention economy, the spectacle of someone eating seventy hot dogs in ten minutes, it seems, is exactly the kind of content that travels.

paragraphs.A: The current record holder can consume over seventy hot dogs in that time — a figure that seems almost impossible.
paragraphs.B: However, the organised modern sport as we know it emerged primarily in the United States during the 1990s.
paragraphs.C: Prize money at the top events can reach tens of thousands of dollars, making competitive eating a viable profession for the best performers.
paragraphs.D: Online broadcasts have brought competitive eating to a global audience of millions.
paragraphs.E: Mental focus is as essential as physical preparation for maintaining pace and composure during a contest.
paragraphs.F: Others contend that there is no strong evidence of lasting harm in experienced, healthy competitors.
paragraphs.G: Scientists have taken a keen interest in the physical characteristics that allow elite eaters to consume such quantities.

questions.explanation: A ofrece el dato concreto del récord actual de perritos calientes, añadiendo el detalle impactante que justifica la descripción del espectáculo.
B introduce el contraste entre los concursos históricos y el deporte organizado moderno, conectando con la creación de la Major League Eating.
C introduce el interés científico en las capacidades físicas de los competidores, preparando la explicación sobre la expansión del estómago.
E añade la dimensión mental del entrenamiento, complementando la descripción física previa y la estrategia que sigue.
F presenta la postura médica contraria, que equilibra la preocupación anterior con la ausencia de evidencia sólida de daño.
D explica el crecimiento de la audiencia gracias a las plataformas en línea, cerrando el artículo con el éxito mediático del deporte.
